words.definition: The latest time by which a task or piece of work must be completed
words.example: We need to submit the full report before the deadline on Friday.

words.definition: A firm promise or obligation to carry out a particular course of action
words.example: She showed outstanding commitment to finishing the project on time.

words.definition: A significant event or stage that marks progress in a project
words.example: Completing the first chapter was an important milestone for the team.

words.definition: Information or comments given to help someone improve their work
words.example: My manager gave me very useful feedback on my quarterly presentation.

words.definition: A specific goal or result that you are working towards
words.example: Our main objective this quarter is to reduce operational costs by 15%.

words.definition: Something that is more important than other things and should be done first
words.example: Getting enough rest should be a top priority during a busy project.

words.definition: To give a task or responsibility to someone else who has the right skills
words.example: Effective managers know how to delegate tasks without micromanaging.

words.definition: The final result or consequence that follows from an action or process
words.example: The outcome of the negotiation was far better than anyone had expected.

words.definition: A further action taken after an earlier one to check progress or results
words.example: I will send a follow-up email to confirm the details of our meeting.

words.definition: Responsible for your own actions and expected to explain or justify them
words.example: Each team member is accountable for their own individual results.

words.definition: Open and honest, sharing information clearly without hiding anything
words.example: Good leaders should always be transparent about their key decisions.

words.definition: The ability to start new tasks independently, without being told to
words.example: He took the initiative to reorganise the team meetings.

words.definition: To delay or postpone doing something that needs to be done
words.example: Try not to procrastinate; break large tasks into smaller, manageable steps.

words.definition: A standard or reference point used to measure or assess performance
words.example: Use last year's sales figures as a benchmark for this year's targets.

words.definition: Taking action to prevent problems rather than simply reacting to them
words.example: Being proactive about your health can help prevent many future problems.
